code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a fluent sentence that summarises all given triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # map predicates to a readable phrase (verb or relation)
    verb_map = {
        "author": "was written by",
        "isbnNumber": "has ISBN",
        "followedBy": "is followed by",
        "language": "is written in",
        "mediaType": "is available as",
        "numberOfPages": "has",
        "precededBy": "was preceded by",
        "country": "originates from",
        "almaMater": "graduated from",
        "doctoralAdvisor": "was advised by",
        "nationality": "is a citizen of",
        "residence": "resides in",
        "codenCode": "has CODEN",
        "issnNumber": "has ISSN",
        "LCCN_number": "has LCCN number",
        "abbreviation": "is abbreviated as",
        "academicDiscipline": "belongs to the discipline of",
        "publisher": "was published by",
        "firstPublicationYear": "was first published in",
        "editor": "was edited by",
        "impactFactor": "has an impact factor of",
        "oclcNumber": "has OCLC number",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": "has LCC classification",
        "genre": "is of genre",
        "literaryGenre": "is a literary genre",
        "eissnNumber": "has eISSN",
        "frequency": "is released",
        "headquarter": "is headquartered in",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "city": "is located in the city of",
        "president": "has president",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "spokenIn": "is spoken in",
        "influencedBy": "was influenced by",
        "family": "belongs to the family",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "location": "is located in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "notableWork": "is notable for",
        "parentCompany": "is a subsidiary of",
        "regionServed": "serves region",
        "founder": "was founded by",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "demonym": "people are called",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "nickname": "has nickname",
        "genus": "belongs to genus",
        "releaseDate": "was released on",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
    }

    # Group predicates by their subject to create compact relative clauses
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, t.predicate)
        # Special handling for a few predicates
        if t.predicate == "numberOfPages":
            phrase = f"{verb} {t.object} pages"
        else:
            phrase = f"{verb} {t.object}"
        subject_map[t.subject].append(phrase)

    # Build sentence parts for each subject
    parts = []
    for i, (subj, phrases) in enumerate(subject_map.items()):
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            part = f"{subj} {phrases[0]}"
        else:
            # Join multiple predicates for the same subject with commas and "and"
            joined = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + f", and {phrases[-1]}"
            part = f"{subj} {joined}"
        parts.append(part)

    # Combine all subject parts into one complex sentence
    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + f", and {parts[-1]}."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: WrittenWork. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:
